title: Clickhouse REST Catalog: AWS Iceberg Rest Catalog Issue
description:
### Company or project name
_No response_
### Describe the unexpected behaviour
After reading the following document and leveraging the following code snippet:
`
CREATE DATABASE my_glue_rest_catalog
ENGINE = DataLakeCatalog('https://glue.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/iceberg')
SETTINGS
catalog_type = 'rest',
warehouse = ''
region = 'us-east-1',
aws_access_key_id = '',
aws_secret_access_key = '', auth_header = '', vended_credentials=true;
`
I'm getting the following error message of missing authentication token:
Code: 86. DB::Exception: Received from localhost:9000. DB::HTTPException. DB::HTTPException: Received error from remote server https://glue.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/iceberg/v1/config?warehouse=s3%3A%2F%2Ftannamfinalnamespace%2Ffirst_table_namespace%2F. HTTP status code: 403 'Forbidden', body length: 42 bytes, body: '{"message":"Missing Authentication Token"}'. (RECEIVED_ERROR_FROM_REMOTE_IO_SERVER)
How am I able to pass the missing authentication when leveraging the AWS Glue Iceberg Rest Catalog API
### How to reproduce
`
CREATE DATABASE my_glue_rest_catalog
ENGINE = DataLakeCatalog('https://glue.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/iceberg')
SETTINGS
catalog_type = 'rest',
warehouse = ''
region = 'us-east-1',
aws_access_key_id = '',
aws_secret_access_key = '', auth_header = '', vended_credentials=true;
`
USE DATABASE my_glue_rest_catalog;
SHOW TABLES FROM my_glue_rest_catalog;
### Expected behavior
_No response_

_No response_

key_bug_features: Missing authentication token when accessing AWS Glue Rest Catalog
bug_features_seed: CREATE DATABASE my_test_catalog ENGINE = DataLakeCatalog('https://glue.us-east-1.amazonaws.com/iceberg') SETTINGS catalog_type = 'rest', warehouse = '', region = 'us-east-1', aws_access_key_id = 'test_key', aws_secret_access_key = 'test_secret', auth_header = 'Bearer test_token', vended_credentials=true;
CREATE TABLE my_test_catalog.test_table (id UInt32, name String, created_at DateTime DEFAULT now(), value Float64, is_active Nullable(Bool)) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY id;
INSERT INTO my_test_catalog.test_table (id, name, created_at, value, is_active) VALUES (1, 'Test Name', now(), 123.45, true);
SELECT id, name, value FROM my_test_catalog.test_table WHERE is_active IS NOT NULL;
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM my_test_catalog.test_table WHERE created_at > now() - INTERVAL 1 DAY;
